'They cannot come to our level': Kaif fires back at Amir in explosive rant
description: Mohammad Kaif sharply rebuked Mohammad Amir for his persistent doubts about India's T20 World Cup campaign. Kaif accused Amir of seeking publicity and highlighted Pakistan's loss to the USA, questioning Amir's credibility to critique others. He urged India to ignore such noise, emphasizing that engaging only amplifies the desired attention.

title: ‘A day of hope, peace and unity’: Donald & Melania Trump extend Holi greetings
description: US President Donald Trump extended warm greetings for Holi, recognizing the Hindu festival as a harbinger of spring and a symbol of good triumphing over evil. He conveyed wishes for hope, peace, and unity to all celebrating this vibrant festival of colours. The message underscores Holi's significance in fostering renewal and strengthening community bonds, particularly for the Indian diaspora.

title: Connor McDavid sends clear message as Oilers face key trade deadline
description: Connor McDavid quickly endorsed the Oilers’ trade for Connor Murphy, stressing the need to add talent without subtracting key pieces. With Edmonton firmly in a win now phase and draft picks holding less value, the move reflects urgency around the Stanley Cup chase. Chicago gains a future asset, while the Oilers strengthen their blue line for an immediate playoff push.

India’s biggest weakness exposed? Ex-India keeper raises red flag before England clash
description: Former India wicketkeeper Parthiv Patel expressed concern over the team's fielding standards ahead of their T20 World Cup semifinal against England. Despite reaching the last four, India's catching efficiency is the lowest in the tournament. Patel praised Sanju Samson's match-winning knock and Jasprit Bumrah's crucial wickets, emphasizing the need for early breakthroughs against England's strong batting lineup.

title: Sea is higher than we thought, millions more at risk: study
description: Researchers studied hundreds of scientific studies and hazard assessments, calculating that about 90% of them underestimated baseline coastal water heights by an average of 30 cm, according to a study in the journal Nature

title: Splashed with colours on Holi, woman hurls boiling water on 4-year-old in Nagpur
description: A shocking Holi incident in Nagpur saw a 60-year-old woman, Sindhu Thakre, scald a four-year-old boy with boiling water after he playfully sprayed colour on her. The child, Om Vange, is hospitalized with significant burns. Police have initiated legal action, emphasizing that such reckless acts, even if provoked, will not be tolerated, and are pushing for maximum charges.

description: Petroleum and gas shipments coming from Gulf countries have been impacted, with consignments stuck at the Strait of Hormuz, which is under Iran's control

description: Nepal's upcoming election sees a generational clash as Gen Z voters challenge traditional leadership. Despite facing constitutional crises and internal party dissent, veteran politician KP Sharma Oli, a former dissident who spent years in prison, remains a formidable force. He is now seeking re-election from his long-held constituency, facing a direct challenge from a younger contender.

title: U.S. House committee votes to subpoena Attorney General Bondi to answer questions over the Epstein files

title: Middle East crisis: Trump rates US war effort ‘15 out of 10’
description: US President Trump declared the campaign against Iran "about a 15" on a scale of 10, citing success in decimating Iranian leadership and missile capabilities. He reiterated the goal of preventing nuclear weapons, vowing to continue joint operations with Israel despite escalating regional conflict, rising oil prices, and domestic criticism.

Maharashtra transporters strike: Trucks, buses, taxis to go off roads from Friday
description: Transporters across Maharashtra have announced an indefinite strike starting Thursday midnight, protesting the alleged misuse of the e-challan system and arbitrary penalties. Talks with the transport commissioner failed, leading to the escalation of the protest, which has garnered support from private car owners and school bus operators. The strike aims to address issues like harassment by officers and unjustified fines.

title: Oxford’s Ashmolean Museum returns 16th century bronze sculpture of Thirumangai Alvar
description: The Ashmolean acquired the statue in good faith in 1967. According to the Sotheby’s catalogue, the bronze was sold by the private collector, J.R. Belmont (1886–1981).  There is no information on how the bronze entered his collection.

title: Spain denies U.S. claim of military cooperation on Iran as rift deepens
description: "We will not be complicit in something that is harmful to the world and contrary to our values and interests, simply out of fear of retaliation," Spain's Socialist Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez said after Trump had lashed out at Sanchez's government, calling Spain a "terrible" ally

title: 7 injured as firewalking ritual goes wrong during lunar eclipse at 1,300-year-old Telangana temple
description: Seven devotees sustained burn injuries during the Agnigundala ritual at Sri Parvathi Ramalingeswara Swamy Temple in Nalgonda district. Overcrowding, reportedly double the usual attendance due to a lunar eclipse, led to a stampede-like situation near the firewalking area. Two women with severe burns were transferred to Gandhi Hospital for advanced treatment.

Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.

title: From protests to polls: Nepal to vote after a Gen Z uprising redraws its political map
description: Nepal is at a critical juncture as nearly 19 million voters elect a new House of Representatives. This election is significantly influenced by a youth-led uprising that challenged the political establishment, fueled by resentment over unemployment and perceived elite excess. The vote reflects a generational demand for accountability and change.

title: Draymond Green reacts to Blackpink K-Pop song mentioning his name
description: Golden State Warriors star Draymond Green expressed excitement after being name-dropped by the popular Korean girl group Blackpink. It was in their new song "Me and My." Green, a four-time NBA champion, views the K-Pop mention as a significant validation of his global influence and popularity. He plans to listen to the song with his daughters, who are fans of K-Pop.

title: OpenAI loses 1.5mn subscribers in less than 48 hours after Altman backs deal that Anthropic rejected
description: OpenAI faces a significant user exodus, with over 1.5 million reportedly leaving ChatGPT after agreeing to let the Pentagon use its AI on classified networks. This backlash, fueled by previous contracts and donations, has seen rival Anthropic's Claude chatbot surge in popularity. Users are now seeking ways to export their ChatGPT data and migrate to Claude.
